brand.name: Innerbloom
brand.positioning: Adaptive habit system for real people
visual_personality: soft and warm in light mode

priority_order: current approved landing and application screenshots
approved logo and exported visual assets in Google Drive
historical examples as loose reference only
rules: If campaign styling hints conflict with the current approved visual system, the current approved visual system wins.
Real current screenshots override outdated written descriptions.
Do not invent a new logo or substitute the logo with arbitrary text styling.
Do not use historical campaign pieces as direct templates unless explicitly approved.
The Asset Producer must rely on approved real assets before generating from scratch.

modes.mode_selection_rule: Use the explicitly approved post mode. If no mode is approved, default to dark. Match source screenshots and palette to the selected mode.

primary_logo.wordmark_style: uppercase, widely tracked, refined sans-serif appearance
primary_logo.icon_style: violet and lilac lotus mark with soft tonal variation

usage_rules: Use the real approved logo asset whenever possible.
Prefer the full icon plus wordmark in headers and hero compositions.
Use icon-only only when space is constrained or as a secondary brand mark.
Never recreate the wordmark with a substitute font.
Do not distort proportions, rotate the mark, or recolor it outside the approved violet/lilac system.
Use a light logo treatment on dark backgrounds and the approved violet/dark treatment on light backgrounds.

placement_guidelines.minimum_clear_space: at least one lotus-icon height around the full logo
placement_guidelines.minimum_visibility: logo must remain legible at mobile feed preview size

families.headline: Sora
families.ui_reference: Use typography visible in current product screenshots as the fidelity reference

eyebrow.tone: small uppercase metadata or step labels

rules: Do not use decorative or script fonts.
Do not imitate the logo with arbitrary typography.
Keep text readable at Instagram mobile preview size.
Avoid excessive all-caps outside short labels and eyebrows.
Headlines should feel product-led and editorial, not like generic quote cards.
Violet and lilac are the primary brand accents.
Do not use green as the primary brand colour.
Do not use pure white as the standard light-mode background.
Do not flatten the brand into beige plus dark green aesthetics.
Functional colours must preserve their product meaning.
Use gradients sparingly and only where they reflect the current landing system.

hero_text_gradient.usage: selected emphasis words or short phrases

ambient_background_glow.usage: soft radial depth behind product compositions

prohibitions: Do not apply the hero gradient to every text element.
Do not use full-spectrum rainbow gradients.
Do not use saturated cyberpunk neon fields.

principles: Use real current product screenshots from approved Drive assets.
Preserve truthful UI and visible data.
Screenshots are a primary proof element, not generic decoration.
Match dark screenshots to dark compositions and light screenshots to light compositions unless an intentional approved contrast is required.
allowed_treatments: mask into a restrained device frame
subtle highlight around a key chart or module
dim non-focal areas without obscuring relevant meaning
disallowed_treatments: rewrite UI labels into unrelated concepts
alter chart values or visible metrics
reuse one screenshot as the hero for the entire campaign
use generic device mockups that conflict with the approved visual language
distort screenshots or use unreadable low-resolution crops

daily_energy.goal: Make the chart clearly readable and the hero module without changing its data

tasks.goal: Show real task progress and adaptive difficulty clearly

emotion_chart.goal: Keep the emotional data legible and truthful

general_principles: Use generous negative space with intentional hierarchy.
Balance editorial copy and product evidence.
Every composition must feel unmistakably branded.
Avoid empty layouts that look unfinished.
Use one dominant focal point per asset.
Use rounded cards, restrained borders, subtle glow, and atmospheric depth.

approved_patterns: headline left plus product composition right
centered concise statement plus subtle product proof
full-screen product crop plus minimal editorial framing
single module spotlight with one truthful callout
static_post.branding_presence: subtle but always visible through logo, palette, typography, or product UI

approved_patterns: problem to mechanism to proof to adaptation to CTA
educational sequence using varied but related layouts
rules: Do not repeat the exact same layout on every slide.
Slides must feel like one system without becoming duplicates.
Use progress indicators only when they add clarity.
Use at least two distinct real product states when the subject supports it.

do: Show real UI modules such as Daily Energy, Dashboard, Tasks, DQuest, Emotion Chart, habit detail, and rhythm selection.
Use approved dark assets for dark posts and approved light assets for light posts.
Keep the approved lotus or full logo visible in a refined way.
Use soft glows and ambient depth.
Use the product itself as primary proof.
dont: Create generic motivational quote cards disconnected from the product.
Use muted green and beige as the core brand system.
Replace the real logo with plain text.
Build the entire campaign from one screenshot.
Overuse empty whitespace without compositional intent.
Create generic stock-wellness artwork when real product evidence exists.

mode_matching: dark post uses dark source assets first
light post uses light source assets first
variation_rules: Vary source modules across the campaign.
Do not reuse one screenshot as the hero in more than two produced assets unless explicitly approved.
For carousels, use at least two distinct visual states when relevant.
Do not duplicate the same template across the entire campaign.

production_review_policy.rule: Produce the complete campaign batch defined in campaign.json in one execution. Do not stop after a limited sample set. Human review occurs after the full batch is produced.

brand_checks: headline and CTA aligned with the campaign brief
